testCases.name: Kings at a distance
testCases.description: No pieces on the board except for the two kings.

testCases.name: At least one bishop
testCases.description: The white bishop is direct attacking the black king.

testCases.name: At least one of two pieces
testCases.description: The white queen and knight are direct attacking the black king.

testCases.name: Two pieces (different)
testCases.description: In this position both a pawn and a bishop need to appear at dice.

testCases.name: Two identical pieces (doubles)
testCases.description: In this position two bishops need to appear at dice.

testCases.name: Three identical pieces (triplets)
testCases.description: In this position three knights need to appear at dice.

testCases.name: One of three pieces
testCases.description: When it's favorable for a player if one of the three specific pieces — be it a knight, a bishop, or a queen — appear, the probability of such an event is very high.

testCases.name: One of four pieces
testCases.description: When any of four specific pieces—a knight, bishop, rook, or queen—can appear on the dice, the probability of such an event increases to 96.3%.

testCases.name: One of five pieces
testCases.description: When any of five specific pieces—a knight, bishop, rook, queen, or pawn—can appear on the dice, the probability of such an event increases to 99.54%.

testCases.name: Three different pieces
testCases.description: The probability that three specific pieces will appear on the dice (in any order) is 2.78%.

testCases.name: Two identical pieces + one different piece
testCases.description: The probability of rolling three specific pieces on the dice, where two are identical (a double) and one is different, is 1.39%, regardless of the order in which they appear.

testCases.name: Queen and Bishop threatening the King
testCases.description: The white queen and bishop are threatening the black king.

testCases.name: A bishop and the three queens
testCases.description: The white bishop is direct attacking the black king and three queens can reach the king as well.

testCases.name: Two pieces or queens
testCases.description: In this position both a pawn and a bishop need to appear at dice.
